lesson.menu_desc: WH-questions for meetings with African and European teams -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Asking and Answering Professional Questions in Meetings
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 14 -- WH-Questions (Meetings)

vocab.title: Match each meeting word to its meaning
defs: the list of topics for a meeting
how much work is done, moving forward
a worry or a problem to talk about

reading.rtitle: Getting Ready for the Alignment Meeting
paras: Every month, I have an alignment meeting with the Sucafina teams in Rwanda and in Europe. Before the meeting, I prepare a short agenda and a list of questions. Good questions help me understand the progress of the program and the concerns of each team.

In the meeting, I ask many questions. I ask, 'Who is responsible for the farm data?' and 'How many farms are in the program this year?' I also ask, 'How often do you verify the properties?' and 'Which criteria are the most difficult?' When I need one small detail, I ask, 'What does this number mean?'

My colleagues also ask questions about my region. They ask, 'Where are your farms located?' and 'Are the producers ready for the audit?' Clear questions and clear answers keep everyone on the same topic. At the end, I confirm the next steps and share the information with my manager.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- A1 reading, WH-questions in meetings

gapfill.text: WH-questions ask for information: Who (a person), Where (a place), What (a thing), How many (a number), How often (frequency), Why (a reason), Which (choose from a group). Yes/no questions start with Do/Does or Is/Are: 'Do most farms comply?' 'Are the producers ready?'

bank.label: Useful meeting questions
items: Who is responsible for the farm data?
How many farms are in the program this year?
How often do you verify the properties?
Which criteria are the most difficult?
What does this number mean?
Where are your farms located?
Are the producers ready for the audit?
Do most farms comply with the rules?

lf.title: Which question word fits?
items: A
Who
is responsible for the data? -- asks about a person.
strong

How many
farms are in the program? -- asks about a number.
strong

do you verify the farms? -- asks about frequency.
strong
D
Which

lf.text: Who = person. Where = place. What = thing. How many = number. How often = frequency. Why = reason. Which = choose from a group. Yes/no questions use Do/Does or Is/Are at the start.

items.situation: Ask who is responsible for the farm data.
tips: Who is responsible for the farm data?

items.situation: Ask how many farms are in the program this year.
tips: How many farms are in the program this year?

items.situation: Ask how often the team verifies the properties.
tips: How often do you verify the properties?

items.situation: Ask, yes or no, if the producers are ready for the audit.
tips: Are the producers ready for the audit?

items: Round 1 -- WH-questions
Ask three WH-questions: Who, How many, How often.
Round 2 -- Yes / No
Ask two yes/no questions with Do/Does or Are/Is.
Round 3 -- Answer back
Answer the colleague's questions about your region: Where are your farms? Are the producers ready?
Curveball
The colleague asks: 'Which criteria are the most difficult?' Answer, then ask one question back.

answerkey.title: Reveal the meeting questions
list: People: 'Who is responsible for the farm data?'
Numbers: 'How many farms are in the program?' / 'How often do you verify the properties?'
Choice and detail: 'Which criteria are critical?' / 'What does this number mean?'
Yes/no: 'Are the producers ready for the audit?' / 'Do most farms comply?'
answerkey.note: WH-questions ask for information (who, where, what, how many, how often, why, which). Yes/no questions start with Do/Does or Is/Are.

listenings.text: Let me read the questions on my meeting agenda. First, who is responsible for the farm data this year? Second, how many farms are in the program in the Rwanda region? Third, how often does the team verify the properties? Fourth, which criteria are the most difficult for the producers? And fifth, are the farms ready for the next audit? These questions help me understand the progress and the concerns of each team before we make a decision. Good questions keep everyone on the same topic.

Hi Carlos, this is Chantal from the Sucafina team in Rwanda. Thank you for your questions. Here is some information for the meeting. In our region, there are one hundred and twenty farms in the program this year. We verify the properties twice a year. Most producers are ready for the audit, but a few farms need more documents. I also have a question for you. How often does your team in Brazil check the data? And who is responsible for the final report? See you at the meeting. Thank you, Carlos.

extra_audio.text: First, who is responsible for the farm data? Second, how many farms are in the program this year? Third, how often do you verify the properties? Fourth, which criteria are the most difficult? Finally, are the producers ready for the audit?
